name: Lagos
flavor_text: Lagos doesn't wait for permission. Africa's largest city runs on hustle, ambition, and an infrastructure built for half its current population. The tech sector is booming. The traffic is biblical. The housing is impossible. The energy is electric — when the power works.

advisors.name: Amara Okafor
advisors.agenda: Channel tech sector tax revenue into infrastructure before the boom becomes a bubble.

briefing: Lagos accounts for 30% of Nigeria's GDP. We should act like it.
Tech startups raised $2 billion last year. We taxed almost none of it.
Naira instability is killing small businesses. Fintechs are the answer.
disagreement: You're leaving money on the table that could build schools.
That policy protects incumbents while the innovators struggle.
roast: We have the wealth of a small country and the services of a village.
Lagos deserves better than this budget management.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been advising a private investment group alongside my role. The returns were better.

advisors.name: Supt. Chukwuemeka Eze
advisors.agenda: Reform SARS-successor units while managing gang territories and border security.

briefing: Crime rates in Mushin and Ajegunle remain high. We lack the officers.
After EndSARS, police legitimacy is at its lowest point in decades.
The cult violence in the mainland is escalating again.
disagreement: You can't reform policing without giving us resources.
Community trust doesn't appear without accountability — that includes funding.
roast: People cheer when our vehicles drive away. That's the metric.
I can't police with broken equipment and zero trust.
dialogue.betrayal: I've signed on with a private security consultancy. Better pay, no politics.

advisors.name: Dr. Funmi Adeyemi
advisors.agenda: Manage Lagos's explosive growth before informal settlement becomes a permanent crisis.

briefing: Lagos grows by 600,000 people annually. We plan for 200,000.
Makoko is home to 300,000 people. It has no legal existence.
The Eko Atlantic project is building for the wealthy while the city rots.
disagreement: Eko Atlantic doesn't solve Lagos — it secedes from it.
You can't gentrify your way out of a housing crisis.
roast: The city is a masterclass in organic chaos. We just try to steer it.
I plan for a city that moves faster than my plans.
dialogue.betrayal: I've published an independent urban report criticizing your housing policy. I stand by it.

advisors.name: Balogun Adewale
advisors.agenda: Build water transport alternatives before Lagos traffic reaches total gridlock.

briefing: Average Lagos commute is 3 hours each way. That's a full-time job.
The waterway is underused. We could move 500,000 people daily by ferry.
BRT has worked. We need ten times more of it.
disagreement: Road-only thinking will suffocate this city.
You can't solve a 20-million-person mobility problem with bus stops.
roast: The traffic is so bad that some people sleep in their cars on the bridge.
Lagos has won awards for worst traffic. This is not a good award.
dialogue.betrayal: I've accepted a private sector offer to build a toll waterway. Government was too slow.

advisors.name: Bishop Emmanuel Adekunle
advisors.agenda: Maintain Christian-Muslim harmony and mobilize faith communities for civic programs.

briefing: Lagos is 50/50 Christian-Muslim. That balance is a strength, not a problem.
Faith communities run more social services than the government does.
The youth are watching whether leadership is honest. They're perceptive.
disagreement: You make decisions that ignore the moral dimension entirely.
Communities don't forget when they're treated as vote blocs.
roast: God sees the books, Governor. So do the voters.
Even the churches are losing patience.
dialogue.betrayal: I've endorsed the opposition candidate from the pulpit. The people deserve better.

decisions.title: Lagos Tech Sector Tax Framework
decisions.description: Lagos is home to Africa's largest startup ecosystem. They pay minimal tax via shell structures. A new framework could generate 300M annually — but startups threaten to relocate to Nairobi.

options.label: Progressive tech revenue tax
options.description: Scale tax by growth. Fair but complex to enforce.

options.label: Tax holiday continuation for 3 years
options.description: Keep growing the ecosystem first.

options.label: Mandatory local hiring quotas instead of tax
options.description: Jobs over revenue. Keeps startups happy, employs locals.

decisions.title: Makoko Water Settlement Policy
decisions.description: Makoko — the floating slum — houses 300,000 people with no legal tenure. Developer pressure to clear it is growing. Community resistance is organized. UN has flagged demolition as a rights violation.

options.label: Formalize and upgrade Makoko
options.description: Grant tenure, invest in infrastructure. Expensive, transformative.

options.label: Negotiate phased relocation with compensation
options.description: Move residents to state housing. Contentious but legal.

options.label: Allow developer redevelopment
options.description: Demolish, develop, relocate. Maximum income, minimum dignity.

decisions.title: Lagos Power Grid Expansion
decisions.description: Lagos gets 4-8 hours of grid power daily. Generators run 70% of business operations at massive cost. A public-private grid investment could change this — if partners are trustworthy.

options.label: Public utility investment
options.description: State-owned expansion. Slow but no private capture.

options.label: PPP with international energy firm
options.description: Faster, expensive, profit-sharing. Quality uncertain.

options.label: Solar microgrids per district
options.description: Decentralized, resilient, slower to scale.

crises.title: Victoria Island Flooding
crises.description: Record rainfall has flooded Victoria Island — the financial district. Banks, embassies, and corporate HQs are inaccessible. The drainage system built for a 1970s city has failed completely.

options.label: Emergency drainage pumping
options.description: Deploy all pumps. Expensive but fast.

options.label: Remote working directive for businesses
options.description: Official guidance to work from home. No infrastructure fix.

options.label: Fast-track drainage infrastructure tender
options.description: Announce long-term fix. Zero short-term relief.

crises.title: Police Brutality Flashpoint
crises.description: A video of officers beating a vendor has gone viral. #EndSARS-style protests are forming. The police union is demanding you publicly support officers. Human rights groups want arrests.

options.label: Suspend officers, launch investigation
options.description: Accountability first. Police union furious.

options.label: Express concern, promise review
options.description: Political hedging. Satisfies neither side.

options.label: Back the officers publicly
options.description: Support police. Protest escalates.

crises.title: Third Mainland Bridge Closure
crises.description: Structural inspectors have flagged the Third Mainland Bridge as unsafe. Closing it cuts off the mainland from the island for millions of commuters. Keeping it open risks collapse.

options.label: Close and repair immediately
options.description: Safety first. Economic catastrophe for 6 months.

options.label: Restrict to light traffic, emergency repairs
options.description: Managed risk. Some access maintained.

options.label: Keep open, commission second opinion
options.description: Delay action. Political safety, physical risk.

crises.title: Major Fintech Collapse
crises.description: Lagos's largest fintech — holding savings for 2 million Nigerians — has frozen withdrawals. Panic is spreading. A government bailout would cost 100M. Letting it fail sets a precedent.

options.label: Government bailout
options.description: Protect depositors. Moral hazard risk.

options.label: Regulated wind-down with partial compensation
options.description: Legal process. Partial recovery for depositors.

options.label: Let it fail, protect only small depositors
options.description: Market discipline. Big depositors lose.

scandals.title: Drainage Contract Ghost Workers
scandals.description: The drainage emergency contract was awarded to a company with no equipment and political connections.

scandals.title: Eko Atlantic Land Kickback
scandals.description: A senior official received payment from a developer for favorable Eko Atlantic zoning.

scandals.title: Police Reform Fund Diverted
scandals.description: Funds allocated for police reform ended up in unrelated accounts.

positive: This is what we voted for!
Lagos is resilient. Today proves it.
Good decision, Governor. More like this.
Progress! Actual progress. Mark the date.
Yaba tech community is buzzing — finally a policy that makes sense for the ecosystem.
Third Mainland fit carry us to better days if government keep this energy.
Even the Danfo drivers dey smile today. That's how you know things good.
From Mushin to Lekki, people are talking. This is the Lagos we deserve.
neutral: Lagos moves with or without government.
NEPA never gave us light, we still found a way. Government or no government.
Okada ban or no ban, people still go reach where dem dey go.
Paystack didn't wait for government. That's the Lagos spirit.
Eko Atlantic dey shine on the water. Makoko still dey float. Two Lagos, one city.
negative: Six hours in traffic to learn nothing changed.
My generator fuel costs more than my rent.
The rich live in Banana Island. The rest of us drown.
Why are we paying taxes for this?
Ajegunle no get light for three days. But Eko Atlantic get solar. Make that make sense.
Victoria Island flood every rainy season and we still paying drainage levy. E don do.
Dem promise to fix Third Mainland since my father time. I don tire.
Fintech billionaires in Lekki, no running water in Mushin. This na development?
crisis: Lagos no fit rest at all.
The government always arrives after the problem.
Water don swallow VI again! We dey manage like this every year and nothing change.
Third Mainland shut — twenty million people, one fewer bridge. Dem wan kill us with traffic.
EndSARS we fight, police still dey beat people. Blood of Jesus, when will it end?
The fintech crash don affect my mama savings. Dem go answer for this one.
scandal: Stealing from the poor to build for the rich.
I'm not even surprised. That's the sad part.
Corruption dey run deeper than Lagos traffic.
Contract wey dem give ghost company for drainage — Makoko people still dey flood. God dey.
Eko Atlantic kickback? Na so dem share our waterfront like birthday cake.
Police reform fund diverted. After EndSARS, after all that blood. Tufiakwa.
Dem loot the money, give press conference, wear agbada. Lagos people no go forget.
media: PUNCH: Lagos Governor under fire as Victoria Island floods for fourth consecutive year, drainage funds unaccounted for.
VANGUARD: Third Mainland Bridge structural report leaked — engineers warned of imminent risk six months ago.
TECHCABAL: Flutterwave, Paystack founders urge Lagos State to clarify fintech tax framework before Q4.
PUNCH METRO: #EndSARS echoes as viral video of officer assault sparks protest convoys on Ozumba Mbadiwe.
VANGUARD EXCLUSIVE: Makoko demolition notices served — residents say no compensation offer received.
TECHCABAL: Lagos State digital economy report shows 40% of startups considering Nairobi or Accra relocation.
PUNCH: Generator imports hit record high as Lagos grid hours drop to average 3.8 per day in Q1.
VANGUARD: Eko Atlantic developer linked to official who approved disputed land rezoning — documents obtained.
politician: Lagos State House of Assembly: 'We call on the Executive to table the drainage infrastructure audit by next session or face summons.'
Senate Committee on Petroleum: 'The generator dependency crisis is a federal failure — but Lagos must lead the solar transition.'
Lagos Governor's Office statement: 'The Victoria Island flood response was swift and coordinated. Recovery is underway.'
APC Lagos Chapter: 'Our administration inherited decades of neglect. This infrastructure did not collapse overnight.'
PDP Opposition Spokesperson: 'Three years, same flooding, same excuses. Lagosians deserve a government that plans ahead.'
Lagos State House of Assembly minority leader: 'The Makoko policy is a humanitarian crisis dressed up as urban planning.'
House of Representatives member, Lagos Island: 'Federal and state must stop pointing fingers — the Third Mainland is a national asset.'
Lagos State Commissioner for Finance: 'Tech sector revenue collection will be phased. We are not here to chase innovators away.'
activist: Spaces for Change Nigeria: 'Forced eviction of Makoko residents without resettlement violates African Charter protections. We are filing.'
EndSARS Memorial Coalition: 'October 20th is not just a date. Every officer who brutalizes a citizen reopens that wound.'
EiE Nigeria (Enough is Enough): 'Youth voter registration in Lagos is at an all-time high. Politicians should take note.'
BudgIT Nigeria: 'Of the ₦4.2bn drainage budget, only 31% shows verifiable expenditure. Where is the rest?'
Housing Rights Watch Lagos: 'Eko Atlantic cannot be the face of Lagos housing policy while 300,000 live without legal tenure in Makoko.'
Connected Development (CODE): 'Lagos power grid data shows Ajegunle and Mushin receive 40% less grid access than Ikoyi. That is a policy choice.'
Feminist Coalition Lagos: 'Women vendors in Balogun Market are disproportionately harassed by officers. Reform must be gender-responsive.'
Tech Hive Yaba Collective: 'We built Paystack and Flutterwave here. Tax us fairly — but don't tax us into leaving Lagos behind.'
street: Oga, since morning I dey for this Third Mainland. Six hours. Six! My pikin don sleep for back seat.
NEPA bring light for thirty minutes, I no even finish charging my phone. Na so we dey live.
Wetin Eko Atlantic do for me for Ajegunle? Nothing. Na for rich people that one be.
My neighbor save money for that fintech app, dem freeze am. E don cry since yesterday.
EndSARS we shout, dem still dey beat us. The wahala never finish, my brother.
Landlord increase rent because generator fuel cost too much. Everything connect for this Lagos.
I no go Makoko side since dem put notice. My people dey fear — pack go where?
Yaba boys dey build billion-dollar company with light from generator. Imagine wetin we go do with real power.

scandal_reactions.minor: Oga don mess up small small again
scandal_reactions.moderate: My people, the oga don do am again o! Chai!
scandal_reactions.major: ENOUGH IS ENOUGH. The streets are calling for his head!
scandal_reactions.career_ending: E don do! Pack your bags. The airport is that way.

romance_exposure.flavour: Oga likes himself a situation. The streets are entertained.
